request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. We need to determine if each candidate is "useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge." We reject items that are legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence. Also allowed category_if_not values: null, front_matter, toc, generic_definition, unsupported, duplicate, other.

We are given a list of 40 candidates with evidence quotes and source pages. We must output a JSON object with a "verdicts" array, one per candidate_id, with is_valid_guideline36_knowledge boolean, reason, and category_if_not if not valid.

We need to examine each candidate.

Candidate List:

1. cand_d91cdc4916aae36f: Title "Supply Fan Start/Stop", summary about fan running during modes. Evidence quote is "5.16.11 .) a. S-R-DIFF. The airflow differential between supply air and return air fans required to maintain" – that seems mismatched. Title says 5.16.1.1 but evidence from 5.16.11? Possibly misattributed. But summary seems plausible from guideline: fan runs in Cooldown, Setup, Occupied. Also if perimeter VAV-reheat, runs in Setback/Warmup. This matches typical sequence. Evidence quote is not directly supporting, but might be a truncated snippet. However, the guideline does have such sequences. But we need to check if the evidence supports it. The evidence quote is from a different section and about differential. That's not directly about fan start/stop. So the summary may not be supported by the provided evidence. The evidence quote is about airflow differential, not about start/stop. So likely unsupported. Also it might be a noise extraction. I'll mark as not valid due to unsupported summary (evidence doesn't match). category_if_not: unsupported.

2. cand_494359f0d38e5a7f: "Totalize VAV Box Airflow to Vps", summary: "Totalize current airflow rate from VAV boxes to a software point Vps for ventilation calculations and diagnostics." Evidence: "Totalize current airflow rate from VAV boxes to a software point Vps." This seems directly from guideline. It's operational: totalizing VAV flows to a point. Likely valid. Section 5.16.1.1 indeed includes this. So valid.

3. cand_3b7ac3efaffc108a: "Static Pressure Setpoint Reset using T&R Logic", summary: "Static pressure setpoint is reset using Trim & Respond logic with parameters in Table 5.16.1.2." Evidence: "Static pressure setpoint. Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) using the parameters shown in Table 5.16.1.2." That's from section 5.16.1.2. It's operational. Valid.

4. cand_fe7849b3f91c9596: "SAT Control Loop Enable/Disable", summary: enabled when supply fan proven ON; disabled and output to deadband otherwise. Evidence: "Control loop is enabled when the supply air fan is proven ON, and disabled and output set to deadband (no heating, minimum economizer) otherwise." Valid operational sequence.

5. cand_c2fcafd07650511a: "Cooldown Mode SAT Setpoint", summary: SAT setpoint is Min_ClgSAT. Evidence: "During Cooldown Mode, setpoint shall be Min_ClgSAT." Valid.

6. cand_1fd5512e8b589ede: "Warmup/Setback Mode SAT Setpoint", summary: SAT setpoint is 35°C (95°F). Evidence: "During Warmup Mode and Setback Mode, setpoint shall be 35°C (95°F)." Valid.

7. cand_ccac9ca0140a97bc: "SAT Control Loop Output Mapping", summary: output mapped to sequence heating coil, OA damper, RA damper, cooling coil. Evidence: "Supply air temperature shall be controlled to setpoint using a control loop whose output is mapped to sequence the heating coil (if applicable), outdoor air damper, return air damper, and cooling coil as shown in Figure 5.16.2.3." Valid.

8. cand_f5edc3dfe561fa78: "Minimum Outdoor Air Damper Open/Close (Separate Damper, DP Control)", summary: Open when supply fan proven ON, Occupied Mode, and MinDPsp > 0; close otherwise. Evidence matches: "Open minimum outdoor air damper when the supply air fan is proven ON and the system is in Occupied Mode and MinDPsp is greater than zero. Damper shall be closed otherwise." Valid.

9. cand_f1b46d2e10beb4e7: "Minimum Outdoor Air Control Disable Sequence for Units with Return Fans (DP Control)", summary: When disabled, fully open economizer damper, set MaxRA-P to 100%, return control to SAT loop. Evidence: "When minimum outdoor air control is disabled: i. Economizer outdoor air damper shall be fully opened. ii. MaxRA-P shall be set to 100%. iii. Economizer and return air damper positions shall be controlled by the SAT control loop per Section 5.16.2." Valid.

10. cand_98be617bdce5328a: "Minimum Outdoor Air Damper Open/Close (Separate Damper, AFMS Control)", summary: Open when supply fan proven ON, AHU in Occupied Mode, and MinOAsp > 0; close otherwise. Evidence: "Open the minimum outdoor air damper when the supply fan is proven ON, the AHU is in Occupied Mode and MinOAsp is greater than zero. Minimum outdoor air damper shall be closed otherwise." Valid.

11. cand_62aab5d8e5787c6d: "Minimum Outdoor Air Control Disable Sequence for Units with Return Fans (AFMS Control)", summary similar to DP control disable. Evidence: "When minimum outdoor air control is disabled: i. Economizer outdoor air damper shall be fully opened. ii. MaxRA-P shall be set to 100%. iii. Economizer and return air damper positions shall be controlled by the SAT control loop per Section 5.16.2." Valid.

12. cand_b29f6d4c83bf2031: "Minimum Outdoor Air Control Loop Enable/Disable (Single Common Damper, AFMS)", summary: enabled when supply fan proven ON and AHU in Occupied Mode; disabled and output set to zero otherwise. Evidence: "Minimum outdoor air control loop is enabled when the supply fan is proven ON and the AHU is in Occupied Mode, and disabled and output set to zero otherwise." Valid.

13. cand_edb25033893a001f: "Minimum Outdoor Air Control Release for Units with Return Fans (Single Common Damper, AFMS)", summary: If economizer high limit conditions not exceeded for 10 minutes or unit not in Occupied Mode, release return damper to SAT control loop. Evidence: "If the economizer high limit conditions in Section 5.1.17 are not exceeded for 10 minutes, or the unit is no longer in Occupied Mode, release return damper to control by the SAT control loop (i.e. return damper position is limited by MaxRA-P endpoint, but is not directly controlled to equal MaxRA-P)." Valid.

14. cand_64be574f73148f8e: "Relief Damper Control (Without Fans)", summary: enabled when associated supply fan proven ON; disabled otherwise. Evidence: "Relief dampers shall be enabled when the associated supply fan is proven ON, and disabled otherwise." Valid.

15. cand_f0771a6608e9f652: "Relief Damper Modulation (Without Fans)", summary: When enabled, P-only control loop to maintain 12 Pa building static pressure; close when disabled. Evidence: "When enabled, use a P-only control loop to modulate relief dampers to maintain 12 Pa (0.05 in. of water) building static pressure. Close damper when disabled." Valid.

16. cand_a968521bb9b7d991: "Relief Fan Lead/Lag Alternation", summary: Relief fans lead/lag alternated per Section 5.1.15.3. Evidence: "Relief fans shall be lead/lag alternated per Section 5.1.15.3." Valid.

17. cand_4e25515345e2b81a: "Relief Fan Group Control", summary: grouped and controlled as one system, same speed, same control loop. Evidence: "All operating relief fans that serve a pressure zone shall be grouped and controlled as if they were one system, running at the same speed when enabled and using the same control loop, even if they are associated with different AHUs." Valid.

18. cand_13a5a5c69fa3c6f4: "Relief Fan Enable/Disable", summary: enabled when associated supply fan proven on; disabled otherwise. Evidence: "A relief fan shall be enabled when its associated supply fan is proven on, and shall be disabled otherwise." Valid.

19. cand_5a140dc645340067: "Relief Fan P-Only Control Loop", summary: single P-only control loop per pressure zone maintains building pressure at 12 Pa. Enabled when any supply fan in pressure zone proven ON; disabled output zero otherwise. Evidence: "5.16.9.6.  A single P-only control lo op for each pressure zone shall  maintains the building pressure at a" - truncated, but seems valid. The summary says setpoint 12 Pa, which is typical. Valid.

20. cand_c8403067268be91a: "Relief Fan Staging - Stage 0 (Barometric Relief)", summary: when relief system enabled and control loop output >5%, open motorized dampers to all enabled relief fans; close dampers when loop output drops to 0% for 5 minutes. Evidence matches exactly. Valid.

21. cand_55384d7ee68c9a44: "Relief Fan Staging - Stage Up", summary: detailed sequence for stage up. Evidence matches. Valid.

22. cand_6f4dd1dc7c0e1d8d: "Relief Fan Staging - Stage Down", summary: detailed sequence for stage down. Evidence matches. Valid.

23. cand_afb2c993d23d20ff: "Return Fan Operation (Direct Building Pressure Control)", summary: Return fan operates when associated supply fan proven ON; off otherwise. Evidence: "Return fan operates whenever the associated supply fan is proven ON and shall be off otherwise." Valid.

24. cand_2963117d075542f8: "Return Fan Control to Discharge Static Pressure Setpoint", summary: Return fans controlled to maintain return-fan discharge static pressure at setpoint. Evidence: "Return fans shall be controlled to maintain return-fan discharge static pressure at setpoint (Section 5.16.10.5)." Valid.

25. cand_df49ead47db8acaa: "Building Pressure Control Loop for Return Fan (Direct Building Pressure)", summary: single P-only control loop per pressure zone modulates to maintain 12 Pa. Loop enabled when supply and return fans proven ON and minimum OA damper open. Exhaust dampers closed otherwise. 0-50% loop output: modulate exhaust dampers; 51-100%: reset return-fan discharge static pressure setpoint. Evidence: "5.16.10.5 ). 5.16.10.4.  Building  static pressure shall be time averaged with a sliding 5 -minute window and 15 second" - truncated, but does mention time averaging. The summary seems to capture the sequence. Possibly valid. But evidence is incomplete, but likely from section 5.16.10.5. The summary mentions exhaust dampers, but the evidence doesn't. However, it's plausible, but the evidence quote is garbled. It says "5.16.10.5 ). 5.16.10.4. Building static pressure shall be time averaged with a sliding 5-minute window and 15 second" - it cuts off. It might not support all details. Yet, the summary is detailed, but the provided evidence is not sufficient to verify. The candidate_id likely corresponds to a valid extraction but the evidence quote is poorly extracted. But we must judge based on provided evidence. The evidence is insufficient to support the full summary. So might be unsupported. However, the summary includes "exhaust dampers closed otherwise" etc. Not in evidence. So reject as unsupported. category_if_not: unsupported.

26. cand_3eca31cf79f7e988: "Return Fan Operation (Airflow Tracking Control)", summary: Return fan operates whenever associated supply fan proven ON. Evidence: "Return fan operates whenever associated supply fan is proven ON." Valid.

27. cand_621011bb2fa4831c: "Return Fan Speed Control (Airflow Tracking)", summary: Return-fan speed controlled to maintain return airflow equal to supply airflow less differential S-R-DIFF*. Evidence: "Return-fan speed shall be controlled to maintain return airflow equal to supply airflow less differential S-R-DIFF*. Where multiple air handling units share a common return fan (i.e. dual fan dual duct), return fan speed shall be controlled to maintain return airflow equal to total supply airflow of all associated units less differential S-R-DIFF*." Valid.

28. cand_ae3456a90ad32a47: "Supply Fan Airflow Limiting (Airflow Tracking)", summary: Supply fan airflow limited by reverse-acting P-only loop with setpoint (Vrf-max + S-R-DIFF*), output max supply fan speed 0-100%. Evidence: "Supply fan airflow shall be limited by a reverse-acting P-only loop whose setpoint is (Vrf-max + S-R-DIFF*) and whose output is maximum supply fan speed ranging from 0% to 100%." Valid.

29. cand_c268e77e8c8162aa: "Relief/Exhaust Damper Control (Airflow Tracking)", summary: enabled when supply and return fans proven ON; closed otherwise; dampers modulate inverse of return air damper. Evidence: "Relief/exhaust dampers shall be enabled when the associated supply and return fans are proven ON and closed otherwise. Exhaust dampers shall modulate as the inverse of the return air damper per Section 5.16.2.3." Valid.

30. cand_9faa0d6fb65ef307: "Freeze Protection Stage 1", summary detailed sequence. Evidence: "If the supply air temperature drops below 4.4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes." Valid.

31. cand_e944b8fe4e532822: "Freeze Protection Stage 2", summary: If SAT drops below 3.3°C for 5 min, fully close both economizer and min OA dampers for 1 hour, set Level 3 alarm. After 1 hour resume min OA and enter Stage 1. Evidence: "5.16.12.2.  If the supply air temperature drops below 3.3°C (38°F) for 5 minutes, fully close both the" - truncated. The summary seems to match typical sequence. The evidence says "fully close both the" likely continues. But it doesn't include the 1 hour and alarm. Might be sufficient? Possibly unsupported because evidence is incomplete. But the title is "Freeze Protection Stage 2" and it's a specific sequence. However, we need to check if the summary is fully supported. The evidence quote is from 5.16.12.2, so likely it contains the full sequence. But the provided quote is cut off. So we can't confirm the 1 hour and alarm. This might be a case of insufficient evidence. I'll consider it unsupported. category_if_not: unsupported.

32. cand_38ecb4d639bd259b: "Freeze Protection Stage 3", summary detailed. Evidence: "5.16.12.3.  Upon signal from the freeze -stat (if installed ), or if supply air temperature drops below 3.3°C" - truncated. Again incomplete evidence. The summary is long, evidence doesn't cover it. So unsupported.

33. cand_876bbdbcf5cee8ee: "Maintenance Interval Alarm", summary: Level 4 alarm when fan operated >1500 hours. Reset when acknowledged. Evidence: "Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval count when alarm is acknowledged." Valid fault diagnostic rule. It's operational knowledge.

34. cand_62246ae11ef14315: "Fan Status Mismatch Alarm", summary: alarm when status differs from command for 15 sec. Commanded on/status off: Level 2. Commanded off/status on: Level 4. Evidence: "Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2 b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4" Valid.

35. cand_fa37a5250e4a627c: "Filter Pressure Drop Alarm", summary: Level 4 alarm when filter pressure drop exceeds larger of alarm limit or 12.5 Pa for 10 min and airflow exceeds 20%. Alarm limit varies: DPx = DP100 * (x)^1.4. Evidence: "5.16.13.3. Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit  or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when" - truncated. Doesn't include the airflow >20% and the formula. So unsupported.

36. cand_1bb71d4f535771f8: "High Building Pressure Alarm", summary: Level 3 alarm when building pressure exceeds 25 Pa for 5 min. Evidence: "High building pressure (more than 25 Pa [0.10 in. of water]) for 5 minutes: Level 3." Valid.

37. cand_7815a2bba29c02da: "Low Building Pressure Alarm", summary: Level 4 alarm when building pressure negative (<0 Pa) for 5 min. Evidence: "Low building pressure (less than 0 Pa [0.0 in. of water], i.e., negative) for 5 minutes: Level 4." Valid.

38. cand_1477b3c532bbaa9d: "AFDD FC#1: Duct Static Pressure Too Low with Fan at Full Speed", summary: Fault condition with DSP avg < DSPSP - ƐDSP and VFD speed >= 99% - ƐVFDSPD. Applies to OS #1-#5. Evidence: "FC#1 Equation: DSP AVG < DSPSP - ƐDSP and VFDSPD ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description: Duct static pressure is too low with fan at full speed Possible Diagnosis: Problem with VFD, Mechanical problem with fan, Fan undersized, SAT Setpoint too high (too much zone demand)" Valid fault diagnostic rule.

39. cand_c37a796764162100: "AFDD FC#2: MAT Too Low", summary: condition MAT avg + ƐMAT < min[(RAT avg - ƐRAT), (OAT avg - ƐOAT)]. Evidence: "FC#2 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation: MAT AVG + ƐMAT < min[(RAT AVG - ƐRAT), (OAT AVG - ƐOAT)] Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description: MAT too low; should be between OAT and RAT Possible Diagnosis: RAT sensor error, MAT sensor error, OAT sensor error" Valid.

40. cand_ae385ff6da847787: "AFDD FC#3: MAT Too High", summary: MAT avg - ƐMAT > max[(RAT avg + ƐRAT), (OAT avg + ƐOAT)]. Evidence: "FC#3 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation: MAT AVG - ƐMAT > max[(RAT AVG + ƐRAT), (OAT AVG + ƐOAT)] Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description: MAT too high; should be between OAT and RAT Possible Diagnosis: RAT sensor error, MAT sensor error, OAT sensor error" Valid.

41. cand_77ed710263a24071: "AFDD FC#7: SAT Too Low in Full Heating", summary: SAT avg < SATSP - ƐSAT and HC ≥ 99%. Applies to OS #1. Evidence: "FC#7 (omit if no heating coil) Equation: SAT AVG < SATSP - ƐSAT and HC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #1 Description: SAT too low in full heating Possible Diagnosis: SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve leaking or stuck open, Heating coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, Fouled or undersized heating coil, HW temperature too low or HW unavailable, Gas or electric heat unavailable, DX cooling stuck on, Leaking or stuck economizer damper or actuator" Valid.

42. cand_731dc0c1b1add504: "AFDD FC#13: SAT Too High in Full Cooling", summary: SAT avg > SATSP + ƐSAT and CC ≥ 99%. Evidence: "FC#13 Equation: SAT AVG > SATSP + ƐSAT and CC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #3, #4 Description: SAT too high in full cooling Possible Diagnosis: SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, Fouled or undersized cooling coil, CHW temperature too high or CHW unavailable, DX cooling unavailable, Gas or electric heat stuck on, Heating coil valve leaking or stuck open" Valid.

Now, we need to decide for each candidate. The ones with truncated evidence: cand_d91cdc4916aae36f (mismatched evidence), cand_df49ead47db8acaa (truncated, details missing), cand_e944b8fe4e532822 (truncated), cand_38ecb4d639bd259b (truncated), cand_fa37a5250e4a627c (truncated). For these, we mark as not valid with category "unsupported" because the evidence does not support the summary.

Also, note that some candidates might be duplicates, but I don't see obvious duplicates. Candidate 9 and 11 seem similar but for DP vs AFMS control, they are distinct. Candidate 14 and 18 are different aspects. Candidate 19 is a P-only loop, but summary is detailed though evidence truncated: "5.16.9.6. A single P-only control loop for each pressure zone shall maintains the building pressure at a" – the summary says 12 Pa. The evidence doesn't show the setpoint. Is it unsupported? The evidence is from page 129, and 5.16.9.6 likely states the setpoint. But the provided quote doesn't include it. We have to judge based on what's provided. So for cand_5a140dc645340067, evidence doesn't mention 12 Pa. So unsupported. Similarly, cand_c8403067268be91a has full evidence, so valid. cand_55384d7ee68c9a44 has full evidence, valid. cand_6f4dd1dc7c0e1d8d has full evidence, valid. So I'll mark the ones with incomplete evidence as unsupported.

For cand_d91cdc4916aae36f: evidence is completely unrelated, so unsupported.

For cand_df49ead47db8acaa: evidence is truncated and doesn't cover exhaust dampers and setpoint reset range. unsupported.

For cand_e944b8fe4e532822: evidence truncated, missing 1 hour and alarm. unsupported.

For cand_38ecb4d639bd259b: evidence truncated, missing details. unsupported.

For cand_fa37a5250e4a627c: evidence truncated, missing formula. unsupported.

For cand_5a140dc645340067: evidence truncated, missing 12 Pa. unsupported.

All others have evidence that directly supports the summary or at least the key point. Some evidence is sentence fragments but still conveys the essential condition, e.g., "Minimum outdoor air control loop is enabled when..." is clear.

Thus, I will output JSON with verdicts. I'll include a short reason for each. For valid ones, reason can be "Evidence supports operational sequence." For invalid ones, reason should mention that evidence is insufficient or mismatched.

Now, produce JSON.
